title: Blocks, ItemStacks & Materials
description: Learn step-by-step how to work with blocks, items, and materials with full explanations, structure, and reasoning.

lessons.title: Blocks Basics
lessons.content:
# Blocks Basics (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: Open Your Project
Open IntelliJ
→ Go to your plugin project
→ Navigate to:

src/main/java/your/package/Main.java (or your command class)

You will be writing code INSIDE a method (for now assume inside a command or event).

## Step 2: Understanding What a Block Is
A Block is ANY block in the world:
- Stone
- Dirt
- Diamond block

Minecraft stores blocks at locations.

## Step 3: Code (Place a Block)

```java
Location loc = player.getLocation();
loc.getBlock().setType(Material.DIAMOND_BLOCK);
```

## Step 4: FULL Breakdown (Line-by-Line)

Line 1:
Location loc = player.getLocation();

- Location = a class (type of variable)
- loc = variable name
- = assigns value
- player.getLocation() = gets player's current position

Line 2:
loc.getBlock().setType(Material.DIAMOND_BLOCK);

Break it down:
- loc.getBlock()
  → gets the block at that location

- .setType(...)
  → changes what block it is

- Material.DIAMOND_BLOCK
  → specific block type

## Step 5: WHERE This Code Goes

This MUST be inside a method, for example:
- inside a command
- inside an event

❌ WRONG (outside class)
✔️ CORRECT (inside method)

## Step 6: What Happens In-Game

When run:
→ The block at the player's position becomes a diamond block

## Step 7: Common Mistakes

❌ player is null → causes crash
✔️ Make sure you are using a Player object

❌ Running in console
✔️ This only works for players

## Step 8: Why This Works

Minecraft world = grid of blocks
Location → finds position
getBlock() → gets block
setType() → replaces it

## Mini Challenge
Change it to GOLD_BLOCK instead

## Recap
You:
- Got player location
- Got block
- Changed block type

quiz.question: Method to get a block at a location?
Method to set a block type?

lessons.title: ItemStack Basics
lessons.content:
# ItemStack Basics (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: Where This Goes
Open your command class
→ Inside your command method

## Step 2: What is an ItemStack?

ItemStack = an item in Minecraft

Example:
- 1 diamond
- 5 apples
- 64 dirt

## Step 3: Code

```java
ItemStack diamond = new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND, 5);
player.getInventory().addItem(diamond);
```

## Step 4: FULL Breakdown

Line 1:
ItemStack diamond = new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND, 5);

- ItemStack = class
- diamond = variable name
- new = creates object
- Material.DIAMOND = item type
- 5 = amount

Line 2:
player.getInventory().addItem(diamond);

- getInventory() → gets player's inventory
- addItem() → adds item

## Step 5: What Happens

Player receives 5 diamonds

## Step 6: Edge Case

Inventory full:
→ Item drops on ground

## Step 7: Beginner Mistake

❌ Forgetting 'new'
✔️ Always use new ItemStack(...)

## Mini Challenge
Give player 10 apples

## Recap
ItemStack = item
Inventory = storage

lessons.title: Custom Item Names & Lore
lessons.content:
# Custom Items (FULL GUIDE)

## Step 1: Where to Write
Inside your command method

## Step 2: Code

```java
ItemStack sword = new ItemStack(Material.DIAMOND_SWORD);
ItemMeta meta = sword.getItemMeta();
meta.setDisplayName("§6Epic Sword");
meta.setLore(Arrays.asList("Sharp and shiny", "Handle with care"));
sword.setItemMeta(meta);
player.getInventory().addItem(sword);
```

## Step 3: FULL Breakdown

ItemMeta meta = sword.getItemMeta();
→ Gets extra data of item

meta.setDisplayName(...)
→ Sets custom name

§6 = gold color

meta.setLore(...)
→ Adds description lines

sword.setItemMeta(meta);
→ IMPORTANT: applies changes

## Step 4: Common Mistake

❌ Forgetting setItemMeta → NOTHING changes

## Step 5: What Happens

Player gets:
- Custom named sword
- With lore text

## Step 6: Why This Exists

ItemStack = base item
ItemMeta = extra data

## Mini Challenge
Change name color to red (§c)
